summary: Matched two character-select-screen .data globals, mnCharSel_803F0A48 and mnCharSel_803F0DFC, by defining CSS icon/mode and bottom-door data structures in mn/types.h, forward-declaring them, and initializing the exact data in mncharsel.c. The PR mattered because the author identified these data blocks as heavily reused by other mncharsel functions and likely to accelerate CSS decompilation. Testing evidence from the PR says objdiff showed 100% match and proper sizing, with static write mods used to verify character mappings, names, and offsets.

key_files.role: Added the matched .data definitions for CSSIconsData mnCharSel_803F0A48 and CSSDoorsData mnCharSel_803F0DFC, including icon rows, mode info, door data, tag/KO-star related data, exact float literals, byte strings, and address comments.
Added CSS structs and enums needed to type the data blobs: CSSModeInfo, CSSIconHud, CSSIconState, CSSIconJointId, CSSIcon, CSSIconsData, CSSDoor, CSSTagData, CSSTag, CSSKOStar, and CSSDoorsData.
Added forward declarations for the new CSS-related structs so the menu headers can reference the new types cleanly.

what_changed: Introduced CSSIconsData mnCharSel_803F0A48 in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, covering the Mr. Game & Watch name bytes, 24 mode_info entries, and the CSS icon table.

Introduced CSSDoorsData mnCharSel_803F0DFC in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, covering four player doors at the bottom of the CSS plus associated tag, name-list, KO-star, joint, string, and coordinate data.
Added float macro constants for reused CSS icon row heights and column bounds, with comments noting they could not be enums because they are floats.
Included ft/forward.h in mncharsel.c so character-kind constants used by the icon data were available.
Added typed CSS data layout definitions in mn/types.h with offset comments and explanatory field comments where the author had confidence.

Added CSS icon enums for HUD indices, icon lock/display state, and joint IDs, while documenting that CSSIconHud is not the same as CharacterKind because the icon list omits characters such as Sheik and Master Hand.
Added baselib/sislib.h include in mn/types.h to support TextGlyphTexture references in the new CSS tag and KO-star structs.
Forward-declared all new CSS structs in mn/forward.h.
smart_moves: Converted raw .data blobs into named, structured initializers rather than leaving them as anonymous bytes, making later mncharsel function work easier to type and read.
Separated the two globals according to observed responsibility: mnCharSel_803F0A48 for icon/mode data and mnCharSel_803F0DFC for bottom-screen doors.
Used exact float literal values such as -24.399999618530273F and 30.200000762939453F where needed for data matching instead of rounding to nicer-looking coordinates.
Factored repeated icon grid coordinates into ICONROWHT_* and ICONBNDS_* macros, reducing repetition without losing the exact binary float values.
Annotated many struct fields with offsets and behavioral notes, which makes the new types useful for future reverse engineering rather than merely satisfying objdiff.
Cross-checked names and offsets using static write mods, not just objdiff, which is especially valuable for UI mapping tables where semantic names can easily be wrong.
Kept uncertain fields as x-prefixed placeholders in CSSDoorsData rather than overclaiming field names where the evidence was incomplete.
decomp_lessons: For large menu .data matches, first recover the memory layout as C structs and then fill a typed initializer in the section where objdiff says the data lives.
Data matches can materially unblock function decompilation when the same globals are heavily referenced across a subsystem.
Use exact compiler/binary float spellings for coordinate tables; rounded UI coordinates can break data matching.
When semantic identity is available from mods, Gecko codes, tools, or in-game static write tests, use it to name fields and enum values, but preserve placeholders for unknown fields.
Offset comments beside struct fields and initializer rows are high-value documentation for later decompilers navigating large UI tables.
Do not assume CSS icon IDs are interchangeable with CharacterKind; this PR explicitly documents a separate CSSIconHud enum because the icon list has a different domain.
Forward declarations belong in forward.h when adding related structs to a subsystem header, keeping type dependencies manageable.
naming_conventions: New CSS types use the CSS prefix: CSSModeInfo, CSSIcon, CSSIconsData, CSSDoor, CSSTagData, CSSTag, CSSKOStar, CSSDoorsData.

CSS icon HUD enum constants use ICONHUD_* with internal character naming consistent with existing Melee identifiers, such as ICONHUD_KOOPA, ICONHUD_MARS, ICONHUD_PURIN, ICONHUD_CLINK, ICONHUD_EMBLEM, and ICONHUD_DRMARIO.
CSS icon joint enum constants use ICONJOINT_* and mirror character/icon names.
CSS icon state enum constants use ICONSTATE_LOCKED, ICONSTATE_TEMP, and ICONSTATE_UNLOCKED.
Reusable icon coordinate macros use ICONROWHT_* for row heights and ICONBNDS_* for column bounds.
Unknown or not-yet-understood fields in CSSDoorsData and related structs retain x-offset-style names such as xc0, xcf, xd7, and x190 rather than speculative names.
Struct field names favor UI behavior where known, such as selected_since_load, p_kind_prev, sel_icon_prev, dooranim_timer, slideranim_timer, is_hold_cpu_slider, and is_hold_handicap_slider.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Objdiff was used to verify 100% match and proper sizing for both data objects.
The author placed the definitions in .data based on objdiff location evidence.
Exact binary-compatible float constants were preserved in the initializer and repeated constants were macroed without changing their values.
Static write mods were used to validate character mappings, names, and offsets in-game or against runtime behavior.
The C layout uses explicit u8, u16, int, float, pointer fields, arrays, and padding/unknown fields to reproduce the original memory layout.
review_feedback: The author noted they failed the first formatting check, then fixed formatting and redid the commits.
No review comments were present in the slice.
A maintainer/commenter response from ribbanya said: "Thank you!"
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use CSSIconsData or CSSDoorsData in mncharsel function matching.
Search for replacements of x-prefixed fields in CSSDoorsData, CSSTagData, CSSTag, and CSSKOStar.
Search for later changes to CSSIconState naming because the field comment and enum order may need confirmation against usage.
Search mncharsel.c for functions referencing mnCharSel_803F0A48 and mnCharSel_803F0DFC after this PR.
Look for PRs that validate or rename CSSIconHud versus CharacterKind mappings.
